channel.title: Vpc on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Vpc on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: AWS InstanceLimitExceeded / VpcLimitExceeded
item.description:
The InstanceLimitExceeded or VpcLimitExceeded error occurs when you try to create more EC2 instances or VPCs than your AWS account allows. Default limits are set per region and can be increased via a quota request.

Common Causes

  • The account has reached the default EC2 instance or VPC limit
  • Previous instances are in terminated state but still count toward the limit
  • VPC limit includes subnets, route tables, and internet gateways
  • New AWS accounts have lower default quotas

How to Fix

Check current EC2 instance limits:

item.title: AWS VPCIdNotSupported / InvalidVPCID
item.description:
The VPCIdNotSupported or InvalidVPCID error occurs when an AWS API call references a VPC ID that does not exist, is in a different account, or is in a different region. This often happens when resources are provisioned with hardcoded VPC IDs.

Common Causes

  • The VPC ID is from a different AWS account or region
  • The VPC was deleted and the ID is still referenced in configuration
  • Typo in the VPC ID format (must be vpc- followed by hex characters)
  • Cross-account resource sharing is not configured

How to Fix

Verify the VPC exists and note its ID:

item.title: GCP Network Error: The Network Has Been Lost
item.description:
The Network error: The network has been lost error occurs when a Google Cloud service loses network connectivity. This can affect Cloud Run, GKE, Compute Engine, and other services that depend on VPC networking.

Common Causes

  • VPC firewall rules blocking required ports
  • Cloud NAT not configured for private instances to reach the internet
  • DNS resolution failure for private DNS zones
  • Network peering configuration is missing or broken
  • The instance’s network interface is misconfigured

How to Fix

Check firewall rules:
